titleStmt.title: Postcard from George D. Roche to Nancy McCarthy, 28 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: George D. Roche

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
In this postcard, George D. Roche writes to his niece Nancy to reassure her that he is OK and that there are no rats where he is “but where they do live they are BIG and no mistake”. He promises to try to get to Cork next time he is on leave.
Nancy McCarthy was a well-practiced letter-writer, and her collection of correspondence spans eighty years. This postcard is one of seven included in the Letters of 1916 collection written to Nancy by her uncle, George D. Roche, on active service with the 7th London regiment during World War I.

msIdentifier.repository: UCC Library Special Collections and Archives

correspAction.placeName: The Convent, Mountrath, Queen's County, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Please Sir, our 'Erbert aint no need to worry
about Conscription 'as he —?
BL/PP/NMC/65 No Active Service Miss Nancy McCarthy
The Convent
Mountrath
Queen's Co.
Ireland 28/2/16 Dear Nancy, Yes I am O.K. Thankee but
very very busy. No rats where I am
but where they do live they are BIG
and no mistake. I will not
forget about the snapshot as soon
as I can get tuk. Plenty of
snow falling lately but it seems
to be all turning to slush now
Owing to the strike on the boats
from Fishguard I could not get
across to Cork within my leave
time last week so stayed in
London. Will try next time Love
from Uncle Gerry
